description: Identify the 'Missing Middle' housing gap in San Francisco using UrbanSim data in the Jupyter notebook at /home/ga/urbansim_projects/notebooks/missing_middle.ipynb. Using the buildings and parcels data from /home/ga/urbansim_projects/data/sanfran_public.h5, analyze residential unit distributions by zone. Categorize units into 'Single-Family' (exactly 1 unit), 'Missing Middle' (2 to 19 units), and 'High-Density' (20 or more units). For zones with at least 100 total residential units, calculate the percentage of units in each category and flag opportunity zones where pct_single_family > 50.0 and pct_missing_middle < 15.0. Export the zone-level results to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/missing_middle_zones.csv with columns: zone_id, total_units, pct_single_family, pct_missing_middle, pct_high_density, and is_opportunity_zone. Save a stacked bar chart showing the housing breakdown for the top 20 opportunity zones (sorted by total units descending) to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/opportunity_zones_chart.png.
